sourceMovie.title: Dogma
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dogma (1999) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kevin Smith's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tenacious D in The Pick of Destiny
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dogma clicked for you, Tenacious D in The Pick of Destiny (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Idle Hands
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dogma clicked for you, Idle Hands (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dogma?
faq.answer: Try Tenacious D in The Pick of Destiny, Idle Hands, Futurama: Bender's Game—each shares DNA with Dogma while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dogma?
faq.answer: Dogma (1999) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dogma worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
